mnemonics.title: Gradient Identity记忆
mnemonics.content: NABLA: N-orm squared gives 2x, A-x gives constant, B-ackprop uses chain rule, L-og derivative is ∇f/f, A-dd product rule: f∇g+g∇f

mnemonics.title: Hessian Nature记忆
mnemonics.content: POSITIVE definite = hill bottom (minimum), NEGATIVE definite = hill top (maximum), INDEFINITE = saddle (neither)

mnemonics.title: Quadratic Form记忆
mnemonics.content: For xᵀAx → think '2A' when A is symmetric; when A is not symmetric, think '(A+Aᵀ)x'

acronyms.usage: Used in ∂/∂x to denote gradient vector of a scalar function
Used to analyze curvature and nature of critical points in optimization

acronyms.expansion: Jacobian matrix - first-order partial derivatives of vector function
acronyms.usage: Generalizes gradient to vector-valued functions; used in chain rule for vector functions
Measures rate of change of f in direction u, computed as ∇f · u

visualTips.visualization: Imagine arrows pointing uphill on a mountain - gradient arrows point in steepest ascent direction at each point, with length proportional to steepness
Think of parabola y = x² in 1D, derivative is 2x. In n-dimensions, the function ||x||² is like x₁² + x₂² + ..., gradient doubles each component
Imagine bending a sheet - Hessian tells how surface curves in different directions; positive definite = bowl shape (minimum), negative definite = inverted bowl (maximum)
Ball rolling downhill following negative gradient - takes steps proportional to steepness, learning rate controls step size

analogies.analogy: Like a compass needle pointing uphill - always points to steepest ascent, like water flowing downhill follows steepest path
Like step size when hiking - too small = slow progress, too large = overshoot the minimum and may never converge
Like a flat spot on a mountain - could be summit (maximum), valley floor (minimum), or a saddle pass (neither)
Like a relay race - each node passes its gradient to previous nodes, multiplying along the way
